session.user: Load and follow the skill instructions in .claude/skills/pr-feedback-classifier/SKILL.md Arguments: --pr 8922 Return the complete JSON output as your final message.
session.assistant: I'll load the pr-feedback-classifier skill and execute it with the specified arguments.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: pr-feedback-classifier 3→description: > 4→ Fetches and classifies PR review feedback with context isolation. 5→ Returns structured JSON with thread IDs for deterministic resolution. 6→ Use when analyzing PR comments before addressing them. 7→argument-hint: "[--pr <number>] [--include-resolved]" 8→context: fork 9→agent: general-purpose 10→model: sonnet 11→--- 12→ 13→# PR Feedback Classifier 14→ 15→Fetch and classify all PR review feedback for the current branch's PR. 16→ 17→## Arguments 18→ 19→- `--pr <number>`: Target a specific PR by number (default: current branch's PR) 20→- `--include-resolved`: Include resolved threads (for reference) 21→ 22→Check `$ARGUMENTS` for flags. 23→ 24→## Critical Constraints 25→ 26→**DO NOT write Python scripts or any code files.** Classify the data using direct AI reasoning only. Writing code to process JSON is unnecessary and pollutes the filesystem. 27→ 28→## Steps 29→ 30→1. **Fetch PR info and all comments in a single call:**
... [194 lines omitted] ...
130→ "action_summary": "Bot suggestion: add unit tests for error handling paths", 132→ "original_comment": "Consider adding unit tests for the error handling paths in this endpoint" 170→ "error": null 184→## Error Case 198→ "error": "No PR found for branch feature-xyz" 216→ "error": null
session.assistant: Now I'll fetch the PR feedback using the specified PR number (8922):

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: git diff --stat -M -C main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || git diff --stat -M -C origin/main...HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "Could not determine file restructuring"

See dignified-python-core.md: No default parameter values rule.", "created_at": "2026-03-07T22:03:51Z" } ] }, { "id": "PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yzoob", "path": "packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/codespace/real.py", "line": 103, "is_outdated": false, "comments": [ { "author": "github-actions", "body": "**Tripwires Review**: Adding subprocess calls to gateway real.py - Missing integration tests in tests/integration/test_real_codespace.py for get_repo_id() and create_codespace() methods. See docs/learned/architecture/gateway-abc-implementation.md.", "created_at": "2026-03-07T22:04:20Z" }

## ✅ Dignified Code Simplifier Review

**Last updated:** 2026-03-07 14:03:40 PT

No violations found. All code changes follow dignified-python standards.

<details>
<summary>Details</summary>

### Patterns Checked
✅ LBYL over EAFP - No violations found
✅ Pathlib usage - Compliant
✅ O(1) properties - Compliant
✅ Error handling at boundaries - Correct pattern used
✅ Import organization - Correct
✅ Code indentation depth - All within limits

- `tests/unit/cli/commands/codespace/test_setup_cmd.py`: No violations

### Activity Log

Now I'll classify the feedback according to the Comment Classification Model. Let me analyze each thread:

1. **Thread PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yzogQ** (fake.py:38) - Author: github-actions (bot) - Type: Actionable code violation - Issue: Default parameter values in __init__ - Classification: Actionable (code change requested) - Pre-existing: false (human would face same issue in original location, but this is a bot comment on new/modified code) - Complexity: local (single location, line 38)
2. **Thread PRRT_kwDOPxC3hc5yzoob** (real.py:103) - Author: github-actions (bot) - Type: Actionable code violation - Issue: Missing integration tests for new subprocess-calling methods - Classification: Actionable (bot suggestion to add tests) - Pre-existing: false (issue is specific to new methods, not pre-existing) - Complexity: single_file (multiple additions in one file, test additions)
